* * *

UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
FEDERAL TRADE COMMISSION
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20580

September 30, 2021

Re: FOIA-2021-01356
FOIA Logs 2014 - 2021

This is in response to your request dated September 21, 2021, under the Freedom of Information Act seeking access to a copy of the FTC FOIA Case Logs covering the time frame January 1, 2014 through the present. In accordance with the FOIA and agency policy, we have searched our records on September 28, 2021.

We have located five spreadsheets of responsive records. I am granting partial access to the accessible records. Portions of these pages fall within one or more of the exemptions to the FOIA’s disclosure requirements, as explained below.

I am denying access to the names of FOIA requesters seeking information on themselves. This information is exempt from release under FOIA Exemption 6, 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6), because individuals’ right to privacy outweighs the general public’s interest in seeing personal identifying information. See The Lakin Law Firm v. FTC, 352 F.3d 1122 (7th Cir. 2003).

If you have any questions about the way we handled your request or about the FOIA regulations or procedures, please contact Chip Taylor at 202-326-3258.

If you are not satisfied with this response to your request, you may appeal by writing to Freedom of Information Act Appeal, Office of the General Counsel, Federal Trade Commission, 600 Pennsylvania Avenue, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20580, or via email at [FOIAAppeal@ftc.gov](mailto:FOIAAppeal@ftc.gov), within 90 days of the date of this letter. Please enclose a copy of your original request and a copy of this response.

You also may seek dispute resolution services from the FTC FOIA Public Liaison Richard Gold via telephone at 202-326-3355 or via e-mail at [rgold@ftc.gov](mailto:rgold@ftc.gov), or from the Office of Government Information Services via email at [ogis@nara.gov](mailto:ogis@nara.gov), via fax at 202-741-5769, or via mail at Office of Government Information Services (OGIS). National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, MD 20740. Please note that the FOIA Public Liaison’s role relates to comments, questions or concerns that a FOIA Requester may have with or about the FOIA Response. The FOIA Public Liaison’s role does not relate to taking action in matters of private controversy nor can he resolve individual complaints.

Sincerely,

Dione J. Stearns
Assistant General Counsel

| FOIA-2019-00656 | This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. $522), I am a journalist based in New York. I request that information pertaining to the following records be provided to me. Copies of any correspondence written or electronic, or from F.T.C. officials, staff, or contractors pertaining to the Halwang Clinic, a private cancer-treatment center located in Denmark, Germany, beginning January 1, 2017 through the present day. I have reported on the clinic's fraudulent practices for the online publication Longreads, and have learned through my reporting that the clinic might have been a subject of discussions at the F.T.C. It is in the strong interest of the public to be aware of steps the F.T.C. has taken to look into questions asked by public reporting such as mine, as well any concerns about the Halwang Clinic that the F.T.C. may have shared with third-party businesses, such as crowdfunding platforms. (Date Range for Record Search: From 0/10/2017 To 0/32/2019) | Gellman, Lindsay | - | Closed |

FOIA-2020-00774 5/11/2020 Dear Sir or Madam, Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act 5 U.S.C. § 522 et seq. I respectfully request that the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to provide me with a copy of all interlinking related complaints that were lodged with Consumer Sentinel (or any successor consumer complaint database) over the past six months (if any) that mention either (a) DISH Network, or (b) another entity that is alleged to have marked DISH Network photos or services. I am waiting to pay fees up of $200. If you expect that the fees will exceed this amount, please contact me before proceeding. Please send all available records to my attention at Mr. Zachary Parker Risk, Audit & Compliance DISH Network L.L.C. 9601 South Meridian Boulevard Englewood, CO 80112 if you need to discuss this request or require further information. I can be reached at 301-729-2881. Thank you for your consideration of my request. Sincerely, Zach Parker (Date Range for Record Search: From 12/11/2019 To 05/11/2020)

Request ID Received Date Request Description Requester Category Requester Name Organization Closed Date
FOIA-2020-00605 3/19/2020 Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), the institute for Public Representation (IPR) at Geogetown Law Center requests the following records in the FTC's possession, custody, or control, including records prepared by consultants or contractors and records provided to the FTC by third parties. All documents relating to clearance requests filed by former FTC employees under 18 C.F.R. § 4.10(2). Clearance requests are requests from former employees of the Commission for permission to work on matters that raise potential or perceived conflicts. The requests must contain information listed in § 4.10(4), including whether the proceeding or investigation was pending in the Commission while the former employee served, whether a proceeding or investigation from which such proceeding or investigation directly resulted was pending during the former employee's service, or whether nonexistent documents or information pertaining to the proceeding or investigation in question, came to or would likely have come to the former employee's attention in the course of the employee's duties, and whether the employee left the Commission within the previous three years. The requested records are the clearance request forms, responses given to the requesters, and any records pertaining to the decisions made by the Office of General Counsel and/or the Commissioners in response to fled clearance requests. This request includes notices given to the requisition of the agency's final decision to grant or deny clearance, and any correspondence containing explanations of the agency's decision. Furthermore, IPR is requesting all internal memoranda, correspondence, emails, notes, communications, and other documents related to clearance requests or the clearance rules. This includes communications and letters to or from Congress and/or the Office of Government Ethics about the clearance rules. IPR asks that if the reports are filed electronically, you provide the requested documents in their electronic format as required under FOA. 5 U.S.C. § 552(t)(2). If portions of the record must be provided in paper format and you estimate that trees will exceed 252, please notify IPR before completing the request. If the FTC claims an exemption for all or any of the requested records, we ask that you provide detailed descriptions of all without records and specific explanations of applicable exemptions in compliance with the requirements of

FOIA-2020-00352 1/15/2020 All correspondence to date between the FTC and Wirecard AG and/or its subsidiaries in particular, information as to whether there is any current or pending investigation into Wirecard AG (a German payment processing company), and the details /scope/ potential charges of that investigation. Of particular note - the FTC prosecuted Allied Wallet in 2019, which used Wirecard as an accuser for its payment processes; as there any investigation into Wirecard following this! Additionally, Wirecard bought Call's issuing business in 2017. Are there any FTC investigations into the acquisition, or into that division's subsequent behavior? In sum: any and all investigations into Wirecard, past, current and pending, plus as many details on scope, potential offences, charges and timeline of investigation as possible. Many thanks

| FOIA-2020-00800 | 5/18/2020 | Hello all, This is (b)(6) sending this message though I now go by (b)(6). You may call me (b)(6), I w am writing this letter to file a Freedom of Information Act (FOA) request for records with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC). The basis for this request for records is the FTC's Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Program Status Report. (...) My request for records are the following. 1) Which undergraduate, graduate and law schools have Joseph J. Simmons, Kevin D. Williams, Elizabeth Kraszewski, Teresa Kelsey, Namon Friends and Stephanie Langley previously attended and visited? What are their respective roles and responsibilities with the FTC? How long have they been with the FTC? What are their respective annual salaries with the FTC? 2) What formal and informal ties does the FTC have with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOQ)? Will you join me in moving to call upon all communications in the form of email and postal correspondences employees and legal representatives of the FTC may have exchanged with the EEOC about Management Directive 715 since January 01st, 2010 to be promptly disclosed either to my e-mail or in the alternative my mailing address? 3) Will you join me in moving to call upon all internal complaints filed by employees and legal representatives of the FTC with an agency official responsible for the oversight of EEO program since January 01st, 2010 to be promptly disclosed either to my e-mail or in the alternative my mailing address? Will you join me in moving to call upon all investigations launched as a direct consequence of the submission of an internal complaint with the agency official responsible for the oversight of EEO program since January 01st, 2010 to be promptly disclosed either to my e-mail or in the alternative my mailing address? See attached document above for additional information requesting records. | News Media | Ayele, Michael | Association For The Advancement of Civil Liberties | 5/18/2020 |

| FOIA-2020-00725 | 4/27/2020 | (b)(6) (AACL) (b)(6)Addis Ababa, Ethiopia E-mail: (b)(6)@@gmail.com. (b)(6)@@gmail.com. (b)(6)@email.com Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) Privacy Act (PA) Request for Records Hello. This is (b)(6) sending this e-mail though now go by(b)(6). You may call me (b)(6), I. (b)(6) am writing this letter to file a FOIA/PA request for records with the United States Federal Trade Commission (FTC). 1) Background I have previously visited Martin Luther King Library. I visited MLK's library after I had become a member of the Montgomery County Public Library (MCPL) on January 2015. On November and December 2014, I had become a member of the Los Angeles Public Library (LAPL) and the San Francisco Public Library (SFPL). On January 2014, I had become a member of the Callaway County Public Library and the Missouri River Regional Library (MRRL). 2) Request for Records On September 2015 was declared "missing" by Montgomery County Media Publication Sonia Burke following numerous calls I had made to have marijuana legalized. USA today also declared me "missing". I am not contacted by the media before being declared "missing". Montgomery County Media Publications and U.S.A Today had previously noted that I requested libraries in the Silver Spring, MD, Arlington, VA and Washington D.C area. My request for records are the following. 1) Have either employees and/or legal representatives of the media, law enforcement agencies or public libraries in the Silver Spring, Maryland; Arington, Virginia and/or the District of Columbia ever gone into communication with employees and legal representatives of the FTC about I. (b)(6) for any reasons mentioned in this request for records or for any other ones? If yes, will you join me in moving to call upon all communications exchanged between employees and legal representatives of FTC, representatives of the media and law enforcement agencies to be promptly disclosed either to my e-mail or in the alternative my mailing address? See attached document above for additional information requesting records. | Educational/Non-Scientific | Ayele, Michael | Association For The Advancement of Civil Liberties | 5/4/2020 |

| FOIA-2020-01034 | 8/10/2020 | Dear Sir/Madam, We are requesting details submitted by Apple Inc. under the (6b) order of FTC (FTC Matter No. 09) on April 20, 2021. In particular, we are requesting the following documents that were submitted by Apple Inc (AAPL) pursuant to this order: 1. The name and address of each acquired entity(See 3d in FTC Matter P201201) 2. The Value of assets acquired, including the Value of any rights to any intangible property (4b in FTC P201201) We are looking for information submitted by Apple Inc pursuant to order FTC P201201. The information related to the order was required to be submitted by technology companies before April 20th, 2020. Since we have not seen the transparency around the results, we are requesting the particulars via this FOIA request. The background of this request pertains specifically to this identified matter. [https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/documents/reports/6b-order-s-file-special-reports-technology-platform-companies/6b\_platform\_study\_sample\_order.pdf](https://www.ftc.gov/system/files/documents/reports/6b-order-s-file-special-reports-technology-platform-companies/6b_platform_study_sample_order.pdf) Please let us know if we can provide any additional details to expedite our request. (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2020 To 08/10/2020) | News Media | ka, Sam | - | 8/10/2020 |

| FOIA-2020-01048 | 8/13/2020 | In August 2019, I filed a Complaint with the District of Columbia Board of Veterinary Medicine (“Board”) against Genevieve Schlorth, DVM with regard to the care provided to my dog, Betsey that resulted in her death. I never received a letter, a document, an update, a phone call—nothing for almost a year. On March 10, 2020 Vito DelVento forwarded me a draft document stating Based upon its review of the relevant veterinary and legal documents, the Board has concluded that no violations of the Health Occupations Revision Act, D.C. Official Code § 3-2012.01, et sez, has occurred. The Board of Veterinary Medicine has now closed the complaint that you filled in this matter. I am requesting a copy of any and all documents, including but not limited to the investigative findings and written report associated with this Complaint. | FOIA-2020-00529 | 3/2/2020 | Dear Sir/Madam: On behalf of my full-time employer Warren Communications News, an independent journalistic publisher of daily publications including Communications Daily. I request under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, the following documents. I'm seeking all letters sent from Chairman Joe Simons and/or the four FTC Commissioners to congressional offices between Feb. 1, 2020 and Feb. 29, 2020. This includes any letters sent from any of the five FTC members to Senator or House office during the month of February 2020. In order to help determine fees, you should know that I am a member of a news organization. As such, please waive all associated fees. If you expect fees, please contact me before proceeding. I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government and is not primarily in my commercial interest. As a member of the media, my purpose is to inform the public. If you need to discuss this request, I can be reached at 202-704-4738. Thank you for your consideration of my request. Sincerely, Karl Herchenroeder Assistant Editor Communications Daily 2115 Ward Court NW Washington, DC 20037 202-704-4738 (Date Range for Record Search: From 02/01/2020 To 02/29/2020) | News Media | Herchenroeder, Keral | Communications Daily | 3/5/2020 |

| FOIA-2021-00218 | To Whom It May Concern, Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records: I request all Consumer Fraud Complaints made against the following websites for the date range listed-below. I also request the disposition of the complaint, if that information is recorded by the Federal Communications Commission. Websites In Which Complaints Are Requested: 1 -- [www.arrests.org](http://www.arrests.org/) 2 -- florida.arrests.org 3 -- texas.arrests.org 4 -- [www.mugshots.com](http://www.mugshots.com/) The date range of this request is January 1st, 2015 to December 11th, 2020. This request is part of a project I am completing on the complaints made regarding Mugshot Publication Websites. I do not request a fee waiver in regard to this request. However, I do request that the Federal Trade Commission notify me before incurring any fees in regard to this request. I also do not request Expedited Processing in regard to this request. However, I request that the Federal Trade Commission provide an acknowledgment of this request upon its receipt of it. Please release the records via email and the online PAL Portal. Please contact the undersigned, via email, if you have any questions regarding the request. I will not respond to phone calls, as I request all communication regarding this request to be written in case of the need for an appeal. I look forward to your cooperation regarding this request. Respectfully Submitted, Mr. Hayden Hodges (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2015 To 12/11/2020) | Hodges, Hayden | Hayden Hodges | Closed |

FOIA-2021-00745 I had my wallet stolen back in November 2007 when I lived in Denver, Colorado & reported this to the FTC at that time. I am trying to get an extended fraud alert with the credit bureau & they're requiring a copy of the identity theft report. I don't recall if it set up an account at the time but I am not able to access this online now at [www.identitytheft.gov](http://www.identitytheft.gov/) as it's not finding my e-mail address. (Date Range for Record Search: From 1/10/2007 To 1/10/2030)

| FOIA-2021-00302 | I’m following up on a previous FOIA request (completed by the FTC on January 4), in which I viewed complaints made about fake job offer scams in 2020. The most common is called Postal Jobs Source, and it operates via several URLs, including postajbjobsourcews.com and [www.postajbjobsourcempd.com](http://www.postajbjobsourcempd.com/). I am requesting copies of any complaints about Postal Jobs Source filed in 2020, to review this further, in the public interest. | FOIA-2021-01217 | To the FOIA Officer. This is a request for records under the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C.§ 552. We hereby request copies of the following records, including both electronic and paper records dated April 1, 2021 and later.(1) Copies of all correspondence, documents, data or other records, along with attachments, sent between the Federal Trade Commission and any other government agency, including but not limited to the Department of Justice, concerning the proposed acquisition of Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Studios Inc.(MGM) by Amazon.com.Inc(Amazon);(2) Copies of all documents or other records, including attachments, constituting or concerning communication with,or submissions by,private parties,including corporate,public interest,elected officials,or other organizations or individuals,concerning the proposed acquisition of MGM by Amazon;(3) Copies of all documents or other records concerning the consideration of the proposed acquisition of MGM by Amazon;(4) Copies of all documents,including but not limited to memoranda,studies,articles,and data,concerning the basis for the consideration of the proposed acquisition of MGM by Amazon.In the event that any records or portions thereof are withheld,please specify the exception under which the agency asserts the records may be withheld.In the event that portions of responsive records are withheld,please release portions of the records that are not exempt and that can be sequestered from the except portions,and indicate where the withheld portions appear on the records.If records are withheld in their entirety,please provide a reasonable estimate of the amount of information that is being withheld,unless giving an estimate would harm an interest protected by the exemption invoked.If you expect the costs of this request to exceed $100,please notify me before proceeding.If you have any questions regarding this request,please contact me at(929)287- | McKeon,Harvey | The Strategic Organizing Center | Closed |

| FOIA-2021-01238 | I request a copy of all documents collected during the FTC's investigation (and subsequent case) of Qualcomm regarding Qualcomm's abuse of their status at SSOs (Standard Setting Organizations) also known as SDOs. I am particularly interested in any documents relating to abuse of their dominant position inside of 3GPP/ETSI one of the largest and most important SSOs. This might including, for example, documents relating to Qualcomm's patenting practices, particularly so called Just In Time' patientting in which several large members of SSOs cut back room deals to get their SEP covered contributions included in the standard even if the contribution isn't positive for the standard. See [https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract\_id=2284024](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2284024). This might also include documents relating to Qualcomm's alleged abuse their Chairmanship of a TSG to bend the rules in their favor. See [http://www.fosspatents.com/2019/07/gaming-system-qualcomm-leverages-3ppg.html#specifics](http://www.fosspatents.com/2019/07/gaming-system-qualcomm-leverages-3ppg.html#specifics) Please consult with a subject matter expert (for example an attorney who worked on the investigation) for this request. | Bez, Sarah | - | Closed |

FOIA-2021-00549 Dear Sir/Madam, By way of background, the Direct Selling Association (DSA) is the national trade association representing companies that sell their products and services by personal presentation and demonstration, primarily in the home. These direct selling companies, with millions of individual American direct sellers, include some of the nation's most well-known commercial names, such as Amway Corporation, Mary Kay Inc, and The Pampersed Chef, Ltd. As part of our continuing efforts to promote the highest ethical standards within the direct selling community, DSA is in the process of reviewing all member companies. We seek information from various business and regulatory entities. Allowed under the Freedom of Information Act, please send me a copy of all investigative records and consumer complaints concerning this company from the past 2 years: Indalo 12/26 SW 13 (Ave Miami, FL 83186-6417) In order to help determine fees, you should know that DSA is a non-profit trade association. If you expect the fees will exceed $100, please contact me before proceeding. If you need to discuss this request, I can be reached at (202) 416-6410. As you prepare the request please take into account that the Direct Selling Association is a registered non-profit. Thank you for your consideration of my request. (Date Range for Record Search: From 01/01/2020 To 02/01/2021)

Campbell, Eleanor Direct Selling Association Closed

| FOIA-2021-01375 | I am seeking access to and copies of all complaints about Coinbase filed with the FTC since 7/1/2021 with information on when received and all narratives I would like to receive the information in digital preferred, thumb drive or CD, as back up. Paper if necessary. I agree to pay reasonable fees for the processing of this request up to $50. Please notify me before incurring any expenses in excess of that amount. For fee categorization purposes, I am a representative of the news media. Through this request, I am gathering information for my journalistic work with CNBC.business television station and website. Accordingly, I am only required to pay for the direct cost of duplication after the first 100 pages. Please waive any applicable fees. Release of the information is not primarily in my commercial interest and will contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and activities, related to cryptocurrency, an area many investors are interested in. Please provide expedited processing of this request which concerns a matter of urgency. This is a breaking news story and we are trying to inform the public within the next 2 weeks. As a business journalist, I am primarily engaged in disseminating information. The public has an urgent need for information about cryptocurrency trading platforms. I certify that my statements concerning the need for expedited processing are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief. If my request is denied in whole or part, please justify all withholdings by reference to specific exemptions and statutes, as applicable. FOIA-2021-01322 Back in 2000-2005 I had a person use my identity to reopen accounts I had paid off following a divorce of sorts. I never closed these accounts but did not use Muriel Pardon was responsible for all the thefts as well as checking account thefts. She ran up approx 82,000 dollars in my name. One day in I believe 2005 I was served and immediately went to sheriff in the report. Without bills they could not give me a report. The company's would not give me charge copies without a police report. I was a mess. In the coming months laws were enforced for identity theft and the East Baton People theft contacted me and a police report was filed. That enabled me to begin the lengthy process of clearing my name. Eaton group ignored all request for information and 9 years Abt 10 months later garnished me for $32,200. They currently have a lease on my home! After lengthy investigation it was determined this was a Circuit City account Muriel Pardon had bought cameras and computer on labeling approximately $200,000 dollars. I am disabled and struggling to move closer to family to help care for me but have to clear this up. I am requesting my identity theft original and all subsequent reports going back to 2002 (Date Range for Record Search: From 09/01/2020 To 01/01/2021)
Kevett, Christine - Closed

FOIA-2021-01340 Dear Federal Trade Commission, Under the Freedom of Information Act, I would like to request the following information: 1. The number of reports made to the FTC in each of the last five years (2016-2020) relating to gambling fraud. 2. The amount of money lost to gambling fraud, again broken down by each of the last five years (2016-2020). 3. The most common types of betting fraud over the last five years, if this is possible to be broken down into categories/specific offences. If any of the above is unavailable or would take the request outside of the cost threshold for an FOI request, please just provide me with the information that is reasonably attainable. All the best, Jamie Dixon

Accordingly, I am only required to pay for the direct cost of duplication after the first 100 pages. 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(II)(I), 5.552(a)(4)(A)(II)(I). News coverage to inform the public. Request for Expected Processing Please provide expedited processing of this request which concerns a matter of urgency. I certify that my statements concerning the need for expedited processing are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief. FOIA-2021-00474 Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5, U.S.C. 552, and the implementing regulations of your agency . Accountable US requests access to and copies: REPORTS REGARDING POLITICAL APPONTEE BURROWING from requesting releasable copies of reports regarding political appointees whom transferred over to non-political permanent positions within this agency since as early November 1, 2016. If possible, we would prefer to receive this information electronically via e-mail at [FOIA@accountable.us](mailto:FOIA@accountable.us). Alternatively, we prefer PDF files on a USB drive or CD/DVD sent by mail to Accountable US, 1919 M St. NW, Suite 450, Washington, DC 20036. If you have questions or need additional information, please feel free to call me at (312) 772-5218. Conclusion if you have any questions regarding this request, including how to constitute the records sought or appropriate searches to locate them, please contact Accountable US. If my request is denied in whole or part, I ask that you justify all deletions by reference to specific exemptions of the act. Additionally, if any documents are withheld based on the Agency's interpretation of any exemption, Accountable US requests that you provide an index of those documents as required under Vaughn v. Rosen, 484 F 2d 820 (D.C. Cr. 1973; card decked, 451 U.S. 377 (1974). Specifically, this Vaughn index should describe withheld documents with enough specificity to determine whether the material is exempt under the act and must describe each document or portion withheld. Thank you for your assistance. Sincerely, Derek Martin c/o Abraham Payton (Date Range for Record Search: From 11/01/2016 To 02/12/2021)

| FOIA-2021-00169 | The basis for this request for records are the requirements of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA). Kennedy had reportedly said in 1963: Simple justice requires that public funds, to which all taxpayers of all races contribute, not be spent in any fashion which encourages, entreats, subsidizes or results in racial discrimination. What I am requesting for prompt disclosure are (1) details of formal and informal ties existing between the CFPB, the DOE, the DOJ, the Department of the Treasury, the DC.Gov, the FTC, the GAO, and my alma mater Westminster College; (2) all your communications in the form of e-mails and postal correspondence about FERPA; (3) all documents within the possession of the U.S Department of Education (DOE) pertaining to I, W that may have come to your attention; (4) all documents within your possession pertaining to authorized representatives of the Comptroller General of the United States, the Attorney General of the United States, the U.S Secretary of Education as well as local and state educational authorities (such as the DC.Gov and others) having previously had access to my education records; (5) all documents detailing the names, the addresses and times of the people associated with the Comptroller General of the United States, the Attorney General of the United States, the U.S Secretary of Education as well as local and state educational authorities (such as the DC.Gov and others) who may previously had access to my education records and (6) all your communications in the form of e-mails and postal correspondence about FERPA with employees and legal representatives of the CFPB, the DOE, the DOJ, the Department of the Treasury, the DC.Gov, the FTC the GAO, as well as other local and state educational authorities. | FOIA-2021-00601 | This was a loss prevention job for a Human Resources department. The entire job became something else. The job description I applied for ended up not being what the job really was. I have shared this consumer complaint with the Washington attorney general. I am also going to be consulted by a private attorney for a no retaliation agreement and to remove all false reports. My employer went into my case reports and changed my narrative and that is perjury and forgery. I called Richard Gold to ask if the home address for the FOIA profile had to be the same address as the requested document to be sent to my email? Richard Gold said, I can leave the home address for the FOIA profile to match my State ID. Richard Gold also said to include these address details here in the description. The address to my apartment is the same as my home mailing address.
